examples: There are three main modes of perceiving: visual, auditory and kinesthetic.
Kinesthetic - This is experience and hands-on practice that's either real or simulated.
Then when you ask a question, if you're kinesthetic you'll get a feeling of going left or right.
To get to that happy state, you need to find kinesthetic connections with the audience.
Learning-style theory proposes that each of us absorbs and processes information in one of several distinct ways: visual, auditory, tactile or kinesthetic, for example.
